message:
fix(reactor): preserve kqueue bookkeeping on deregister failure; fix chaos IO injection with empty error kinds

KqueueReactor::deregister now reads registration info without removing
it from the map before attempting the kevent EV_DELETE call. If the
kernel delete fails (e.g., because the kqueue fd was temporarily
invalid), the registration is preserved so a subsequent retry can
succeed. The bookkeeping is only removed on success, on ENOENT (filter
already gone), or on EBADF when the target fd itself is closed. This
prevents a leaked registration slot that could never be reclaimed after
a transient kqueue failure.

ChaosConfig::is_enabled and summary now treat io_error_probability > 0
with an empty io_error_kinds list as effectively disabled, since
should_inject_io_error cannot pick an error kind to inject.
ChaosRng::should_inject_io_error short-circuits to false when
io_error_kinds is empty, preventing a downstream panic from choosing
from an empty slice. Test added to verify the three-way consistency
(is_enabled, summary, injection behavior).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
